blocks.text: DTC P1009: VTC system advance angle abnormality

The continuously variable valve timing control mechanism (VTC) hydraulically actuates the VTC actuator to control the intake camshaft phase in order to obtain optimum valve timing The PGM-FI ECU determines a failure if the difference between the intake camshaft phase control command value and the actual intake camshaft If the difference between the shaft phase value and the actual intake camshaft phase value continues for a set period of time at or above the set value, or if the intake camshaft phase value is abnormal, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC
Intake camshaft angle in excess of 10° for 2 seconds after engine restart After more than

blocks.text: When the intake camshaft phase value is 55.6° or higher within the monitored range (intake camshaft phase abnormal advance angle detection) after 4.1 seconds have passed since the abnormal advance angle was detected, or when the intake camshaft phase value remains 20.0° or higher for 4.1 seconds or longer with VTC inactivated.

items.text: VTC hydraulic valve sticking on advance side

blocks.text: Start the engine and let it idle.
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
